title: Grout cleaning paste
subtitle: A bicarbonate and tea tree paste for cleaning tiled grout lines
excerpt: Grout is porous and absorbs mould, soap scum, and mineral deposits over time. Bicarbonate of soda provides mild alkaline abrasion to lift surface deposits. Tea tree essential oil adds antifungal action at a useful concentration. Apply with an old toothbrush, scrub, and rinse.
sourceNotes: Grout cleaning chemistry from The Complete Book of Clean, Lim (2019). Tea tree antifungal efficacy at practical concentrations from Hammer et al., Journal of Applied Microbiology (2006). Bicarbonate formulation from DIY Natural (diynatural.com, May 2026).

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 200 g of cleaning paste, enough for a bathroom's worth of grout
recipe.batchNotes: Make fresh for each cleaning session. The paste does not improve with age. If making ahead, store in a sealed jar.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in advance and store in a sealed jar for up to 3 months. The paste may harden slightly on the surface: press through and use the paste beneath.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months in a sealed jar. Bicarbonate paste may slowly absorb moisture from the air and become crumbly: stir in a few drops of water to restore consistency.

glossaryTerms.definition: Tea tree essential oil (Melaleuca alternifolia) has demonstrated antifungal and antibacterial activity in laboratory studies at concentrations of 1% and above. In a cleaning paste applied to grout, the concentration is high enough to be effective during the contact time. It does not prevent regrowth unless the source of moisture is addressed.

content.text: Grout mould is primarily a moisture management problem. This paste removes visible mould and lightens discoloured grout but will not prevent regrowth if the underlying moisture source: poor ventilation, leaking grouting, or a consistently damp surface: is not addressed. Use this paste monthly as part of a regular bathroom cleaning routine. For severe black mould, a stronger commercial product may be needed first.
Tea tree essential oil is toxic to cats. Ventilate the room during and after use.

depends on adequate contact time: leave the paste on the grout for 5 to 10 minutes before scrubbing. Do not use on unsealed natural stone grout, where the alkalinity of bicarbonate can cause etching over time.

Place the bicarbonate of soda in a bowl. Add the tea tree essential oil and stir through. Add the castile liquid soap gradually, stirring between additions, until a thick paste forms. Transfer to a jar.

To use: apply paste directly to grout lines with an old toothbrush or a grout brush. Leave for 5 to 10 minutes. Scrub firmly along the grout lines. Rinse thoroughly with clean water. The bicarbonate will dissolve completely and leave no residue if rinsed well. Dry the surface to slow mould regrowth.
3 months in a sealed jar. If the paste stiffens, add a few drops of castile soap and stir to restore consistency.
